NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Section

ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples
Docs

CLI Verification Quickstart

A short path from source checkout to validating the first portable-evidence bundle.

The first public verification path uses the Northroot CLI and the github-repo-inspection portable-evidence bundle.

Build

cargo build --workspace

Validate Primitive Files

cargo run -p northroot -- validate examples/github-repo-inspection/actor.json
cargo run -p northroot -- validate examples/github-repo-inspection/obligation.json
cargo run -p northroot -- validate examples/github-repo-inspection/policy.json

Hash And Verify The Receipt

cargo run -p northroot -- hash examples/github-repo-inspection/events.jsonl
cargo run -p northroot -- verify examples/github-repo-inspection/receipt.json \
  --base-dir examples/github-repo-inspection \
  --json

The receipt verifier resolves file:// evidence against the bundle directory, hashes each evidence file, and compares the result to the receipt.

Optional Journal Replay

The source example keeps events in JSONL. A generated .nrj journal is a derived artifact for framed replay:

cargo run -p northroot -- append /tmp/github-repo-inspection.nrj /tmp/event-1.json
cargo run -p northroot -- list /tmp/github-repo-inspection.nrj --json
cargo run -p northroot -- verify /tmp/github-repo-inspection.nrj --json --strict

Implementation Notes

The example intentionally starts with JSON and JSONL. The optional .nrj container is reserved for cases where binary framing, streaming verification, and truncation handling are worth the extra format.

Operational Lessons

The quickstart should remain direct: clone, build, validate, hash, verify. The kernel should prove evidence without requiring a hosted console.

Source

northroot/README.mdnorthroot/examples/github-repo-inspection/README.md

Explanations live here. Canonical contracts remain in source.
